data.title: France's manufacturing PMI falls to 47.80 points in November
data.description:
• Manufacturing PMI in France fell to 47.80 points in November from 48.80 points in October 2025.
  • Manufacturing PMI in France averaged 49.69 points from 2011 until 2025, reaching an all- time high of 59.40 points in May of 2021 and a record low of 31.50 points in April of 2020.
  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWQ)
  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Bowhead Specialty prices $150M debt offering via issuance of senior notes
Inhibikase Therapeutics sells 68.97M shares and pre-funded warrants at $1.45/share
ASOS reports FY results; gives FY26 outlook
data.description:
• ASOS press release (ASOMF): FY Revenue of £2.48B (-14.8% Y/Y).
  • During the 52 weeks to 31 August 2025 (the year) the Group realised an adjusted loss before tax of £98.2m, and an adjusted EBITDA of £131.6m, a £51.5m improvement on the results for the same period last year.
  • Outlook In FY26, enabled by the strategic and financial progress made throughout our turnaround, we expect: • GMV to show an improving trajectory throughout the financial year; • GMV performance 3-4ppts ahead of revenue performance, driven by continued growth of Flexible Fulfilment models. Reflecting this mix shift, we have moved to GMV as the primary indicator of top-line performance; • Further gross margin improvement of at least 100bps to 48% to 50%, driven by continued growth in full-price sales mix and Flexible Fulfilment models; • Further adjusted EBITDA growth to £150m to £180m, supported by continued gross margin expansion, variable contribution and fixed cost discipline, with a meaningful year-on-year margin improvement in both H1 and H2 FY26; and • Broadly neutral free cash flow.

data.title: Babcock International Group GAAP EPS of 28.50p, revenue of £2.54B; reaffirms FY outlook
data.description:
• Babcock International Group press release (BCKIF): 1H GAAP EPS of 28.50p.
  • Revenue of £2.54B (+5.4% Y/Y).
  • Contract backlog: £9.9 billion, reflecting significant Land and Aviation orders in 2H25 (FY25: £10.4 billion).
  • Underlying free cash flow: £141 million, with underlying operating cash conversion of 83%.
  • Net debt excluding leases reduced by £90 million to £56 million, a gearing ratio of 0.2x (FY25: 0.3x).
  • Interim dividend: up 25% to 2.5 pence per share (HY25: 2.0 pence).
  • On track to deliver FY26 target margin of 8% and medium-term target of >9%.
  • FY26 outlook "Our expectations for FY26 are unchanged. We expect to achieve an underlying operating margin of 8%, with good progress to towards the medium-term guidance we set in June 2025: average revenue growth of mid-single digit, underlying margin of at least 9%, and average underlying operating cash conversion of at least 80%."

data.title: OGE Energy prices $345M stock offering
data.description:
OGE Energy (OGE) priced an offering of 8,023,256 shares at $43.00 each, with an underwriter option to buy up to 1,203,488 additional shares.

Morgan Stanley and J.P. Morgan are acting as joint lead bookrunners of the offering and representatives for the underwriters. In connection with the offering, OGE Energy entered into separate forward sale agreements with each of Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC and JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association, New York Branch.

The company has agreed to issue and sell directly 4,011,628 shares to the underwriters in the offering, and the forward sellers agreed to borrow from third parties and sell to such underwriters 4,011,628 shares in connection with the forward sale agreements.

OGE Energy (OGE) plans to use the proceeds it receives from the sale of the shares offered and sold directly to its general funds to fund capital expenditures, including the Horseshoe Lake generating units 13 and 14 and Ft. Smith to Muskogee Transmission line, and for other general corporate purposes, including to repay or refinance debt.

data.title: Veolia to acquire hazardous waste company Clean Earth for $3B
data.description:
• Veolia Environnement (OTCPK:VEOEY) has entered into an agreement with Enviri (NVRI) to acquire Clean Earth, a U.S.-based hazardous waste company, for an enterprise value of $3B (or ~€2.6bn).
  • The transaction is aimed to close in mid-2026. It offers strong value creation for shareholders with $120M of synergies by year 4 and current EPS accretion from year 2.
  • Enviri shareholders are expected to receive cash consideration of $14.50 - $16.50 per share in the transaction and retain full ownership of Harsco Environmental and Rail through a spin-off of those businesses into a standalone publicly traded company.
  • The deal will double Veolia’s U.S. hazardous waste footprint, allowing it to offer a full range of environmental services on a nationwide basis. Upon the completion, the group’s hazardous waste revenue will reach €5.2B, with EBITDA margin of 17%. Veolia also raised the target for its hazardous waste activities, now aiming EBITDA growth of at least 10% over 2024-27.

data.title: Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest buys Nvidia shares for first time in months
data.description:
• Cathie Wood’s flagship fund ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) bought 93,374 Nvidia (NVDA) shares on Thursday, the fund’s first purchase of the chipmaker since Aug. 4, according to ARK’s daily trade disclosures.
  • The move came after Nvidia (NVDA) reported stronger-than-expected earnings. Nvidia shares initially rose more than 5% following the results before reversing to close down 3.15% at $180.64 on Thursday, as broader markets fell and investors scaled back expectations for a December Federal Reserve rate cut.
  • As of November 20, ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) held 505,395 Nvidia shares, representing its 23rd largest position.

data.title: UK retail sales fall 1.1% in October, defying forecasts and marking first drop since May
data.description:
UK retail sales volumes dropped 1.1% month-on-month in October 2025, defying forecasts of -0.1%, following an upwardly revised 0.7% growth in September. 

This marked the first decline since May, as supermarket sales declined for the second straight month.

data.title: Frontline Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 misses by $0.05, revenue of $432.65M beats by $163.63M
data.description:
• Frontline press release (FRO): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.19 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $432.65M (-11.8% Y/Y) beats by $163.63M.
  • Achieved average daily spot time charter equivalent earnings ("TCEs")1 for VLCCs, Suezmax tankers and LR2/Aframax tankers in the third quarter of $34,300, $35,100 and $31,400 per day, respectively.
  • Sold its oldest Suezmax tanker, built in 2011, for a net sales price of $36.4 million and delivered the vessel to its new owner in September 2025. After repayment of existing debt, the transaction generated net cash proceeds of approximately $23.7 million in the third quarter of 2025.
  • "We expect the spot TCEs for the full fourth quarter of 2025 to be lower than the spot TCEs currently contracted, due to the impact of ballast days during the fourth quarter of 2025."
